title: Trump Backs Hungary's Orban in Phone Call Amid Rift with Vance's Rally
body:
In a move that has sparked significant attention in the lead-up to Hungary's national election, former US President Donald Trump has reportedly made a phone call to Viktor Orban, the embattled Hungarian leader, in what appears to be a high-profile endorsement. The development comes as Orban's Fidesz party faces stiff competition in the polls, with Orban's anti-immigration stance and authoritarian policies increasingly under scrutiny.

Orban has long been a key ally of the Trump administration, and the former president has repeatedly praised his stance on immigration, while criticizing the leadership of several major European nations, including the UK, France, and Germany. Trump's vocal support for Orban's policies has been seen as a nod to his own hardline stance on immigration, which has become a central plank of his domestic and foreign policy agenda.

While Trump's phone call to Orban is likely to be seen as a boost to the Hungarian leader's campaign, it remains to be seen what impact it will have on the election. Hungary's national election is set to take place next week, and Orban's Fidesz party is facing fierce competition from a number of opposition parties, including the Socialists and the Momentum movement. Orban's popularity has been waning in recent months, with many Hungarians expressing concerns about the country's democratic backsliding and Orban's increasingly authoritarian style of governance.

Despite the challenges he faces, Orban remains a key figure in European politics, and his relationship with Trump has been a source of significant controversy in recent years. Orban's stance on immigration and his efforts to reassert Hungary's sovereignty have been seen as a bulwark against what he sees as the threat of globalism and the erosion of national identity. Trump's endorsement of Orban's policies has been seen as a reflection of his own views on these issues, and has sparked concerns about the implications for US-Hungary relations in the event of a Biden administration.

The development also raises questions about the future of US-Europe relations, particularly in the context of the ongoing tensions between the US and the European Union over issues such as trade, security, and immigration. As the global landscape continues to shift, it remains to be seen how Trump's endorsement of Orban will impact the delicate balance of power in Europe, and what implications it will have for the US's relationships with its European allies.

As Hungary's national election draws to a close, Trump's phone call to Orban is likely to be a key factor in the campaign, with many Hungarians watching closely to see how the former president's endorsement will play out. While Orban's Fidesz party remains a dominant force in Hungarian politics, the party's fortunes are far from certain, and the outcome of the election remains too close to call. One thing is certain, however: Trump's endorsement of Orban will likely have significant implications for the future of US-Europe relations, and will be closely watched by politicians and pundits around the world.
